name: Mocha Madness Part 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrape the chilled custard into an ice cream maker and freeze according to the manufacturer's instructions. Pack the ice cream into a covered container and freeze for at least 2 or 3 hours, or overnight.
Make the mocha chip ice cream:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine the cream, half-and-half, and espresso beans. Bring to a gentle boil, then remove from the heat.
2. Place cocoa powder in a small bowl.
3. Add 2-3 tablespoons of hot cream mixture to the cocoa powder and stir until smooth paste forms.
4. Return the cocoa powder mixture to the saucepan and whisk until smooth. Cover and allow to infuse for 30 minutes.
5. In a medium bowl, using a wire whisk, blend yolks, sugar, and salt together.
6. Strain cream mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a heavy medium saucepan. Bring to gentle boil over medium heat.
7. Remove from heat; gradually whisk about 1 cup hot cream mixture into beaten egg yolks until blended. Return this mixture back to the saucepan.
8. Continue cooking over medium-low heat, stirring constantly with wooden spoon for 3-5 minutes until custard thickens slightly. Do not boil. It is done when finger leaves path on coated spoon in custard after running through it.
9. Remove pan from heat and strain cooled custard into stainless steel bowl placed over larger bowl of ice water. Stir for 5-10 minutes or until chilled; remove from ice bath and stir vanilla extract in. Cover surface with plastic wrap and refrigerate until very cold (2 hours).
10. Scrape chilled custard into an ice cream maker and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions. Just before ready, add chocolate chips and process until evenly distributed throughout the ice cream.
11. Pack ice cream into a covered container and freeze for at least 2 or 3 hours, or overnight.
Make mocha sauce:
1. Combine chocolate and espresso powder in medium bowl.
2. In small saucepan, combine cream, sugar, and butter over low heat until sugar dissolves completely; let come to gentle boil.
3. Pour cream mixture over chocolate; let sit for 30 seconds to melt the chocolate slightly (sauce can be prepared up to two days ahead). Rewarm in top of double boiler or microwave on medium [50%] power if needed.
4. Layer one scoop of chocolate ice cream at bottom of each parfait glass, followed by small scoop of mocha chip ice cream; drizzle some mocha sauce over serving.
5. Repeat layering process with remaining ingredients and finish topping each parfait with dollop whipped cream and few chocolate-covered coffee beans. Sprinkle cocoa powder on top before serving immediately.
